verification.note: Section 6 day-count rules for FY 2025-26 (AY 2026-27) are governed by the Income Tax Act, 1961 (the Income Tax Act, 2025 only applies from tax years beginning 1 April 2026 onward), confirmed against the Income Tax Department's own Non-Resident FAQ page, which states the basic conditions (182 days; or 60 days + 365 days in preceding 4 years), the crew-member/employment-abroad relaxation (182-day test only), the visiting-citizen/PIO relaxation (182 days, or for income >Rs 15 lakh excluding foreign sources, 120 days + 365 days in preceding 4 years), the Section 6(1A) deemed-resident rule (Indian citizen, income >Rs 15 lakh excluding foreign sources, not liable to tax elsewhere - always classified RNOR), and the RNOR test (non-resident in 9 of preceding 10 years, OR present 729 days or less in preceding 7 years) are all unchanged from the 1961 Act and carry over as continuity tests even once the 2025 Act applies in later years. Only individuals and HUFs can be RNOR.

citizen_leaving_for_employment_or_ship_c.note: 60-day + 365-day condition does not apply at all

visiting_citizen_or_pio_standard.note: Applies when total income excluding foreign sources is Rs 15 lakh or less; above that see high_income_visiting_citizen_or_pio

high_income_visiting_citizen_or_pio.note: 60 days is replaced by 120 days (not removed) when income excluding foreign sources exceeds Rs 15 lakh; if resident only via the 120-179 day band, status is RNOR not ROR
